methods.description: Process for drilling, transporting, and analyzing ice core samples for stable isotopes, dust, major ions, and black carbon.

steps.description: A 181.8 m ice core was drilled to bedrock in September 2009 and shipped in a frozen state to the Lomonosov Moscow State University for preliminary investigation.
steps.searchable_summary: drill and transport ice core to university for analysis

steps.description: The core was analyzed for stable isotopes using discrete samples at the Arctic and Antarctic Research Institute in St. Petersburg, Russia.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze ice core for stable isotopes

steps.description: The core was also sampled using a Continuous Flow Analysis (CFA) system at the Institut des Géosciences de l'Environnement (IGE) in Grenoble, France.
steps.searchable_summary: perform continuous flow analysis on ice core

steps.description: CFA streams were routed for dust, major ions, and black carbon analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: route CFA streams for specific analyses

steps.description: The final CFA stream was collected in 1 L glass jars precleaned with pesticide-grade solvents and refrozen.
steps.searchable_summary: collect and refreeze final CFA stream

steps.description: Samples were later melted in the stainless steel clean-room laboratories for organic analyses and extracted using 200 mg Oasis ® HLB cartridges.
steps.searchable_summary: melt and extract samples for organic analysis

steps.description: In order to obtain appropriate volumes for extractions, four consecutive samples were combined.
Fluxes were calculated as concentrations in ice multiplied for the height of water equivalents and divided by the years of deposition.
Multiple and independent techniques were used to date the core, including annual layer counting, stable isotopes and major ions analyses, cross-checked with radioisotopes and volcanic spikes.
steps.searchable_summary: date ice core using multiple techniques

methods.description: Analysis of physico-chemical properties and concentrations of selected fragrances, with corrections for instrumental response and procedural blanks.

steps.description: Results were corrected using the instrumental response factors and procedural blanks conducted among samples extracting ultrapure water.
steps.searchable_summary: correct results using instrumental response factors

steps.description: The mean method recoveries were calculated for PAHs and fragrances.
The method detection limit was calculated as three times the standard deviation of the blank signal.
Fragrance concentrations below method detection limits in any sample were excluded from the discussion.
